scope_basis: Elsevier describes CES as selectively publishing outstanding research founded on the Science of Chemical Engineering.
best_framing: fundamental fine-particle migration, retention and pore-topology response in packed granular media
current_strengths: DEM parameter matrix; drag-to-weight collapse; pore topology; completed localized-release target rows; bounded cropped-domain OpenFOAM pressure checks
main_risks: pressure response is not validation-grade CFD/LBM/experimental evidence; loading scan is single-seed; source-position/source-inventory law is not fitted
must_keep_boundary: pre-clogging migration/filtering framework; no universal critical transition
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: public repository DOI and admin metadata; validation-grade flow or experimental evidence for stronger performance claims

scope_basis: Elsevier frames IJMF around mass, momentum and energy exchange among phases, including disperse flows, porous media and granular flows.
best_framing: gas-driven particulate transport in a connected packed-bed pore skeleton
current_strengths: gas-drag DEM transport; migration fronts; BTC and retention; localized release evidence
main_risks: one-way Stokes drag does not resolve local gas redistribution; no two-way gas-particle coupling
must_keep_boundary: one-way drag model, not resolved pore-scale multiphase flow
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: OpenFOAM/OpenLB/LBM or validated pore-network pressure-flow comparison

scope_basis: Elsevier positions Computers & Chemical Engineering around modelling, simulation, numerical analysis, optimization, process systems engineering and informatics for chemical-engineering problems.
best_framing: an automated DEM-to-voxel-to-topology-to-surrogate workflow for packed-bed debris-transport risk screening
current_strengths: reproducible pipeline; parameter sweep framework; topology metrics; fractional-tail fitting; surrogate and safe-domain tools
main_risks: the current manuscript is written as a physical mechanism paper, not yet as a computational-methods paper
must_keep_boundary: computational screening framework, not a validated reactor-design tool
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: tighten algorithmic novelty, add workflow validation cases and foreground reproducibility/software architecture

scope_basis: Physics-route option retained as an internal strategy; current manuscript would need a stronger flow/transport physics rewrite.
best_framing: anomalous fine-particle transport, tailing and topology response in a granular pore network
current_strengths: fractional-tail analysis; drag-to-weight migration collapse; pore-network descriptors
main_risks: current flow is not resolved; fusion-blanket motivation must be shortened; physics novelty must dominate application narrative
must_keep_boundary: mechanism-first flow/transport physics, not blanket-design validation
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: stronger anomalous-transport scaling, repeated cases, or pore-flow coupling

scope_basis: Elsevier describes Powder Technology as covering wet and dry particulate systems, particle characterization, packing, flow and permeability of particle assemblies.
best_framing: fine-particle retention, deposition and pore-structure diagnostics in a packed ceramic pebble bed
current_strengths: particle-scale DEM; deposition/blockage profiles; DEM-derived voxel topology; bounded pressure proxies
main_risks: must avoid implying experimental CT, pressure-calibrated safety limits or critical transition
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: author metadata and public data/code DOI; optional 908 target-time result

scope_basis: Elsevier describes Particuology as covering discovery, formulation and engineering of particulate materials, processes and systems, including modelling and particle-fluid systems.
best_framing: particle-level transport and retention mechanisms in granular pore networks
current_strengths: mechanistic particle migration metrics; topology/blockage separation; publication-style figures
main_risks: fusion-specific motivation may need shortening; topology framework must be tied to particle mechanisms
must_keep_boundary: mechanism-first particle systems paper, not blanket-design validation
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: tighten introduction and discussion around general particulate transport

scope_basis: The Society of Powder Technology, Japan describes APT as integrating powder and particulate-material science and technology, including DEM/CFD numerical simulation.
best_framing: numerical powder/debris transport and retention in a packed bed
current_strengths: DEM workflow; compact figures; source-data traceability; reproducible scripts
main_risks: needs clear novelty beyond a DEM case study
must_keep_boundary: workflow and mechanism rather than universal scale law
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: emphasize transferable descriptors: BTC, blockage, connectivity and local conductance

scope_basis: Elsevier describes FED as devoted to fusion energy technology, including theory, models, methods, designs, blankets, materials, tritium fuel cycle and safety.
best_framing: fusion blanket ceramic breeder pebble-bed purge-pathway risk screening
current_strengths: Li4SiO4 material context; 1 mm pebbles; 90 GPa stiffness; blanket-relevant gas-driven debris problem
main_risks: less general powder-science impact; current study does not validate a blanket safety limit
must_keep_boundary: numerical screening framework for blanket-relevant mechanisms
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: expand blanket implications and operating-window discussion

scope_basis: IOP/IAEA position Nuclear Fusion around fusion plasma physics and fusion technology, including reactor concepts and enabling fusion systems.
best_framing: blanket-relevant Li4SiO4 breeder-bed purge-path reliability and debris-induced transport risk
current_strengths: fusion-material context; realistic pebble stiffness; breeder-bed purge-gas debris mechanism
main_risks: the current evidence is a packed-bed DEM transport study and may not be central enough to fusion performance or reactor design
must_keep_boundary: fusion-relevant numerical mechanism study, not a validated blanket performance prediction
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: connect to blanket operating scenarios, tritium extraction impact and ideally pressure-flow or experimental benchmark evidence

scope_basis: Elsevier frames Nuclear Engineering and Design around nuclear-engineering design, safety, analysis and reactor-component studies.
best_framing: numerical risk screening for packed breeder-bed purge-path blockage in nuclear fusion blanket components
current_strengths: safety-relevant obstruction metrics; operating-domain language; Li4SiO4 blanket context
main_risks: journal fit is broader nuclear engineering rather than powder transport; fusion blanket specificity must be made practical
must_keep_boundary: screening-level risk metric, not a licensing-grade safety limit
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: more explicit component-scale implication and uncertainty discussion

scope_basis: Fusion-materials backup retained for Li4SiO4 context, but current evidence is transport/topology rather than material degradation.
best_framing: ceramic breeder pebble-bed degradation and debris-transport risk under purge-gas conditions
current_strengths: Li4SiO4 context; realistic pebble stiffness; DEM-derived structural descriptors
main_risks: materials response is not directly modeled; no irradiation, thermal cycling or experimental degradation data
must_keep_boundary: numerical transport and structure-response study, not a materials degradation experiment
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: stronger breeder-material discussion or coupling to fragmentation/degradation mechanisms

scope_basis: Springer describes the journal as publishing transport phenomena in rigid and deformable porous media, including numerical modelling and packed-bed transport examples.
best_framing: breakthrough, retention and connectivity response of fines in a packed granular porous medium
current_strengths: BTC/retention metrics; DEM-derived porosity and connectivity; anomalous-tail fitting framework
main_risks: application-specific Li4SiO4 framing should be generalized; current pressure-flow evidence is cropped-domain screening rather than validated permeability data
must_keep_boundary: porous-media transport descriptors, not pressure-calibrated permeability closure
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: broader porous-media comparison and stronger transport-model validation

scope_basis: High-impact chemical-engineering route retained as a stretch option; current manuscript lacks validation-grade pressure or experimental evidence for CEJ-style performance claims.
best_framing: application-facing particulate transport risk in packed beds
current_strengths: complete reproducible workflow; fusion-relevant motivation; mechanism figures
main_risks: no experiment, no pressure-calibrated performance metric, and limited process-engineering demonstration for CEJ standards
must_keep_boundary: avoid overselling current data as engineering validation
upgrade_needed_for_best_fit: experimental or high-fidelity CFD validation plus stronger engineering-performance claims
